article_meta.title: close video GasBuddy expert says claims the US will see 'massive spike' in gas prices are 'erroneous' Patrick De Haan, G

reasoning: No attribution violations and one solid catch, but one mislabeled item and several missed instances of author-level bias (e.g., 'historic volume'; vague 'experts warn').

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text is from the author’s prose; no quoted or clearly attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Both flagged passages are defensibly biased in author voice; the second suffers from vague attribution, making it a valid bias area though mislabeled.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear instances such as 'historic volume' (subjective adjective) and vague attributions like 'experts warn', and possibly spin like 'rattle global energy markets.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: First label (Sensationalism) is defensible; second should be Omission of Source Attribution rather than Opinion Presented as Fact.
